skills.pattern: FIFO full flag asserts too early — full at count 15 instead of 16
FIFO count not resetting on rst_n — missing reset clause
skills.fix: Add if (!rst_n) begin count <= 0; wr_ptr <= 0; rd_ptr <= 0; end to always block

skills.pattern: The rst_n input is declared but never used in any sequential always block, leaving all registers in unknown (X) state after reset.
skills.fix: Add synchronous reset condition to all always blocks to initialize wr_ptr, rd_ptr, count, and memory to known values when rst_n is low.

skills.pattern: The FIFO full flag is asserted after 15 writes instead of 16 writes because the full condition checks count == 15 rather than count == 16.
skills.fix: Correct the full threshold in the combinational logic block from 15 to 16 to match the FIFO depth of 16 entries.

skills.pattern: The full flag asserts at count=15 instead of count=16 after 16 writes (FIFO depth is 16).
skills.fix: Change the full flag comparison from `count == 5'd15` to `count == 5'd16` to match the actual FIFO depth of 16 entries.

skills.pattern: The RTL code ignores the rst_n signal entirely, leaving state registers and counters uninitialized, causing simulation to fail on reset tests.
skills.fix: Modify all always @(posedge clk) blocks to include an rst_n condition that resets wr_ptr, rd_ptr, count, and output dout to zero.

skills.pattern: The RTL code sets dout only inside the synchronous block during a read operation, but the output dout is not assigned during reset, leaving it with an unknown value that cannot be converted to an integer.
skills.fix: Add an explicit assignment dout <= 8'd0 inside the reset condition of the always block to initialize the output to 0 during reset.

skills.pattern: The FIFO `full` threshold is incorrectly set to 15 instead of 16, so full asserts one entry early and the count never reaches 16.
skills.fix: Change the full threshold comparison from 5'd15 to 5'd16 to match the FIFO depth of 16.
